races.race_name: Clearanswer Call Centres Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lovers Leap
runners.spotlight: Ran well in similar event at Beverley (8.5f, good to soft; cheekpieces fitted) on seasonal debut, while shaping as if this drop back in distance will prove ideal; respected in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Ran well in similar event at Beverley, with cheekpieces fitted, on seasonal debut

runners.name: Crown Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Ran to only a modest RPR with her midfield effort (beaten 13l) in 1m AW maiden at Newcastle but may do better with the outing/experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Midfield in 1m AW maiden; may do better with the outing/experience under her belt

runners.name: Talking In Kode
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly who has a lot to find on the figures and is likely to be better suited by switching to handicaps soon.
runners.comment: Likely to be better suited by switching to handicaps soon

runners.name: Way Of The Will
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 7f last summer, better effort when modest fourth at Redcar on debut; sole 4yo in this field and needs improvement to beat the best of her younger rivals.
runners.comment: Sole 4yo in the field and needs improvement to beat the best of her younger rivals

runners.name: Longevity
runners.spotlight: Recorded meagre RPRs in her two autumn runs, last of six at Newcastle (7f, AW) latest; handicaps should be more suitable shortly.
runners.comment: Meagre RPRs in two autumn runs; handicaps more suitable shortly

races.race_name: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish "Confined" EBF Novice Stakes (One Run Or Less) (GBB/IRE Inc')

runners.name: Navy Light
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a 6f scorer for his yard; duly showed some promise with keeping-on fourth of seven over that trip at Ripon (good); perhaps not crying out for this drop to 5f but otherwise is the form pick and open to progress to boot.
runners.comment: Showed promise with keeping-on fourth of seven over 6f at Ripon; the form pick

runners.name: Bladey Lady
runners.spotlight: 6,000gns foal, £20,000 yearling; Minzaal half-sister to six winners, notably Fond Words (French 6.5f/1m AW including Listed; RPR 97); dam 7f-1m2f winner (including Listed; 113); one of two newcomers in the field; market instructive.
runners.comment: £20,000 yearling; by Minzaal; one of two newcomers in the field; market helpful

runners.name: Anchiano
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros yearling, 110,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Acclamation half-brother to winners Tuscan Point (5f/6f AW; RPR 74) and Sposto Le Nuvole (Italian 6f 2yo); dam 6f scorer (RPR 85); yard has made a respectable start with 2yos this term; interesting.
runners.comment: 110,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; yard has made a respectable start with 2yos this term
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Suzie May
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always behind in C&D event, having been the rank outsider; best watched.
runners.comment: Slowly away and always behind in C&D event

runners.name: Ronson
runners.spotlight: Out of a 6f winner for his connections; never landed a blow in C&D contest, having been friendless in the betting; possibly one for nurseries over an extra furlong later on.
runners.comment: Never landed a blow in C&D contest, having been friendless in the betting

runners.name: Mr Darling
runners.spotlight: Related to several winners for his stable; green and always towards the rear in C&D maiden last week but may do better with the experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Green and always towards the rear in C&D maiden last week but may do better

races.race_name: Bowel Cancer Screening Programme Saves Lives Selling Stakes

runners.name: Merlier
runners.spotlight: Raced five times, mostly on AW, winning twice (5f); creditable fourth off a mark of 85 in Lingfield handicap (6f) most recently; leading player, provided the form is transferred back to turf.
runners.comment: Raced five times, mostly on AW; leading player provided form is transferred back to turf

runners.name: Saucy Jane
runners.spotlight: Very similar type to Artista, having scored over C&D (good to firm) last May; behind that filly at Bath most recently, running below par, but holds a good chance on these terms provided she rebounds.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last May; good chance on these terms, provided she's in top form
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners

runners.name: Tide Runner
runners.spotlight: Sold twice as a 2yo, fetching 3,000euros then 55,000euros (breeze-up); by Mehmas and first foal of unraced half-sister to Group-placed 7f 2yo winner Bluedrum; sole newcomer in the field; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: 55,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; by Mehmas; sole newcomer in the field; market helpful

runners.name: Artista
runners.spotlight: Won the novice event on this card (good to firm) last year; made the frame in handicaps in last two outings, latest at Bath (5f, firm); possibilities back here, provided she takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Won on this card last year; possibilities back here, provided she takes to headgear

runners.name: Jack Rabbit Slims
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has failed to back up last June's Carlisle effort (6f) and faces a difficult task at the weights in this field; first-time tongue-tie needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Maiden whose chance depends on the effects of first-time tongue-tie

races.race_name: Annie Oxtoby Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Beerwah
runners.spotlight: Prevailed at Redcar (5f, good to firm) most recently, adding to his C&D success last September (both in Class 6); however, he's back up in grade and has the worst of the draw.
runners.comment: Both wins in Class 6, including latest start; back up in grade and has the worst draw

runners.name: Miss Brazen
runners.spotlight: Defied a higher mark over C&D three years ago; modest strike-rate since but regained the winning thread at Nottingham (5f, good) on reappearance and may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Regained the winning thread at Nottingham on reappearance; may remain competitive

runners.name: Dark Kestrel
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner who was unable to take advantage of his lower turf mark last time and it remains three years since his only success in this sphere; others preferred.
runners.comment: Beaten returned to turf last time; still three years since sole win in this sphere

runners.name: Sherlock
runners.spotlight: Ran well, half-length second in big field, at Thirsk (6f, good) returned to turf last time; registered sole success (on AW in February) in his only 5f attempt; no mystery if his tally is doubled.
runners.comment: Ran well over 6f returned to turf last time; gained sole success in his only 5f attempt

runners.name: Without Flaw
runners.spotlight: Ties in with Glory Hyde on Wetherby reappearance running; has done all her winning in Class 6 but she's 2-3 over C&D and could go well from a good draw back here.
runners.comment: All wins in Class 6 but she's 2-3 over C&D and could go well back here

runners.name: Tanjen
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-3 on AW; 0-9 on turf but finished first in her group at Goodwood (5f, good) on seasonal debut and looks a possible danger provided this race pans out  better.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf but finished first in her group at Goodwood on reappearance

runners.name: Dandy Dinmont
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate since 2022 and his form thus far this term hasn't continued the broad consistency he showed in 2025; no certainty to take advantage of a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Judged on 2025 form he's no certainty to take advantage of a reduced mark

runners.name: Fuji Mountain
runners.spotlight: Close second at Pontefract and respectable fourth over C&D since wearing blinkers; losing run is mounting up but he again has frame possibilities in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Close second at Pontefract and respectable fourth over C&D since wearing blinkers

runners.name: Glory Hyde
runners.spotlight: Proved resurgent with neck success at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) most recently and remains competitively treated on peak form, which features a C&D win; likely player from the inside draw.
runners.comment: Won at Wetherby last time and remains competitively treated on peak form; well drawn

runners.name: Jojo Rabbit
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D (off 1lb higher) last summer and at Pontefract this spring but has proved inconsistent otherwise during that period; no banker to give his running.
runners.comment: Has won over C&D but his recent inconsistency is a negative
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr

runners.name: Hover On The Wind
runners.spotlight: Close third, despite traffic issues, in AW contest when last seen two months ago; defied a 2lb higher mark over C&D (good to firm) last May and warrants respect back here.
runners.comment: Close third on AW when last seen; defied a 2lb higher mark over C&D last May; respected

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap

runners.name: Amidst The Chaos
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who completed a double last August; did well to finish third, having met traffic issues, at Ripon (1m, good) with reappearance under his belt; enters the reckoning.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo; did well to finish third at Ripon last time; enters calculations

runners.name: Tees George
runners.spotlight: Proving solid this season and scored over C&D (good to firm) most recently, taking Beverley form figures to 21021 (the duck egg is probably best forgiven); likely to remain competitive.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D most recently, taking Beverley form figures to 21021; solid claims

runners.name: Coolree
runners.spotlight: Prevailed by half a length last week, taking C&D record to 11231; successful in this race for the last two years and commands respect, even under a 4lb penalty, kept to his ideal scenario.
runners.comment: Prevailed last week, taking C&D record to 11231; won this race for the last two years

runners.name: Blufferonthebus
runners.spotlight: Respectable fourth at Newcastle (7f, AW) last time, with reappearance under her belt; has form over C&D but the forecast ground isn't ideal; has gained most turf wins on soft.
runners.comment: Has form over C&D but the forecast fast ground isn't ideal

runners.name: Dr Rio
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is a multiple winner, from 7.5f to 1m4f, at Beverley; however, latest effort (here) doesn't suggest his old legs are crying out for this 3.5f drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Veteran whose latest effort suggests he's not crying out for this drop back in trip

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-9 on turf but she transferred her recent AW consistency back to turf last week, going close from the front at Ayr (1m, good to firm); clear possibilities off the same mark.
runners.comment: Record of 0-9 on turf but she went close at Ayr last week; possibilities off same mark

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over middle distances and she's a dual 1m2f winner at Beverley; placed over C&D on penultimate start but the ground was on the slow side; others preferred.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D on penultimate start; gained both course wins over 1m2f

runners.name: Sir David
runners.spotlight: One win for previous yard; two duck eggs this season for new stable leave a big doubt over his current form; best watched unless the market suggests a resurgence is likely.
runners.comment: Two duck eggs this season for new stable leave a doubt over his current form

runners.name: Sunny Orange
runners.spotlight: Successful four times at Beverley, all in Class 6, most recently on penultimate start; placed in Class 5 at Thirsk latest; could go well but this isn't his ideal grade.
runners.comment: Successful four times at Beverley, albeit in Class 6; not in his ideal grade

runners.name: Superfortress
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden; best efforts when second on AW on two occasions; didn't live up to market expectations last time (handicap debut) but progress is still a possibility.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market expectations last time but progress is still a possibility

runners.name: Not Me
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has posted a couple of fifth-place finishes back on turf this term; needs to raise his game somewhat and best form remains on AW.
runners.comment: Maiden who needs to raise his game; best form remains on AW

runners.name: Betty Bassett
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-12 for Ian Williams, the success off current mark in 1m2f Class 6 last June; still in same ownership; bit to prove in this scenario on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Record of 1-12 for Ian Williams; makes stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Hopeful Joy
runners.spotlight: All starts last July, best effort when close second at Ripon (1m, good) on penultimate; that form isn't strong but she could have something to offer in handicaps; check the market signals.
runners.comment: Best effort isn't strong form but she could have something to offer in handicaps

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Mental Health Handicap

runners.name: Regulus Black
runners.spotlight: Modest RPRs at about 1m but treated accordingly for this handicap debut and going up in trip could help; market moves are awaited with interest.
runners.comment: Modest RPRs at about 1m but treated accordingly for this handicap debut

runners.name: What Fools Believe
runners.spotlight: Appreciated the step up in trip when just prevailing over 1m at Southwell and the first-time cheekpieces fitted when only fifth back there are now removed; she is looking hard to predict, however.
runners.comment: Recent Southwell winner but disappointing there last time and looking hard to predict

runners.name: Captain Bruce
runners.spotlight: Won 7f/1m2f AW handicaps for previous yard but has finished tailed off in both runs this season; changed yards.
runners.comment: Dual winner but has finished tailed off in both runs this season; changed yards

runners.name: Fareenar
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement at two; only 9-2 for handicap debut at Redcar (1m, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) but she was in trouble a good way out.
runners.comment: Only 9-2 for handicap debut at Redcar but she was in trouble a good way out

runners.name: Contemplation
runners.spotlight: 0-4 for James Ferguson and quiet in two runs for current yard, over 1m and 1m2f.
runners.comment: 0-4 for James Ferguson and quiet in two runs for current yard, over 1m and 1m2f

runners.name: Ciao Capo
runners.spotlight: Progressed last season, culminating in a nursery win over 1m at Newcastle; gelded during the winter and his Ripon return was eye-catching; might be ready for this longer trip.
runners.comment: His Ripon return was eye-catching; might be ready for this longer trip

runners.name: Wadacre Geisha
runners.spotlight: No real positives to take from her first three races but entitled to fare better now handicapping off a modest mark; best to let the betting guide.
runners.comment: Poor so far but now goes into handicaps and best to let the betting guide

runners.name: Regal Knight
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in three runs last autumn; only fourth of eight and well held on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m; 11-1); needs to step forward.
runners.comment: Only fourth of eight and well held on handicap debut at Newcastle
runners.owner: D Bishop P Robinson S Bland T Coyle

runners.name: Attention Seeker
runners.spotlight: Has gone backwards since showing promise last season for Harry Eustace; can't be recommended on this year's dismal efforts.
runners.comment: Has gone backwards since showing promise last season for Harry Eustace

races.race_name: Worcester Racecourse Ladies Day 6th June Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Queensbury Boy
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Kelso in February last time to make it 0-4 over fences but that was on heavy going and he's had a wind op since, while the return to a right-handed track is a likely plus; good second at Windsor (3m, soft) in January and he seems fine on good ground; solid claims if back on song.
runners.comment: Good second at Windsor in January & has had wind op since below-par run on heavy at Kelso

runners.name: Court In A Storm
runners.spotlight: All eight of his races have been on good ground; this bumper and two-time novice hurdle winner (including here) has been running pretty well in staying handicap hurdles this spring and he may well improve for going chasing, which was the case with his useful dam Aimigayle.
runners.comment: In pretty good form over hurdles this spring and he may well improve for going chasing

races.race_name: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guarenteed Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Delpotro
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; won twice at Sedgefield last year (good to firm/good; up to extended 2m3f) and returned from a break to finish third of five at Plumpton (2m1f, good to soft) in March, not beaten far; could be tough to reel in.
runners.comment: Returned from break to finish 3rd at Plumpton; this front-runner could be tough to reel in

runners.name: Quick Sharpener
runners.spotlight: Struggled last season on final three runs for the Newland/Insole yard; however, he won off 4lb higher at Bangor (2m4f, good) last April so has a handy mark to work with if a change of scenery has prompted a revival; new yard is enjoying a good month; remains in the same ownership.
runners.comment: Struggled on final three runs but handy mark if change of stables has prompted a revival

runners.name: Beep Beep Becky
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on all four hurdle starts, most recently when 9-1 for her handicap debut at Catterick (3m1f, good) in March; however, this lightly raced point runner-up could be suited by the switch to fences and drop back in trip; a tongue-tie goes on; a market move would be interesting.
runners.comment: Lightly raced point runner-up who could improve for going chasing; watch the betting

races.race_name: Ludford Car Parts Group Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hidden Fortune
runners.spotlight: Point winner (3m, good to yielding) who was runner-up on both bumpers starts (good to soft/good); she's a sister to a 3m hurdle winner and the step up in trip could be a firm positive on her first hurdle start.
runners.comment: 3m point winner for whom the step up in trip could be a firm plus on hurdle debut

runners.name: Lady Kara
runners.spotlight: 0-8, with five of those defeats over hurdles, and it's possible she's vulnerable to stronger stayers at this trip; however, she's run well on both starts this spring and there's every chance that she'll be bang there.
runners.comment: 0-8 but she's been in good form this spring and there's every chance she'll be bang there

runners.name: Kittys Glance
runners.spotlight: Following wind surgery she settled better in a first-time hood (retained) to win at Huntingdon in March on her second hurdle start (2m3f, good; bled from the nose); carries a penalty today but her pedigree provides optimism that she will continue to progress.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon in March and pedigree provides optimism that she will keep progressing

runners.name: Liasse d'Argent
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability when sixth at 200-1 at Stratford (2m, good) on her second start, but not enough to suggest she'll pose a threat today.
runners.comment: Showed some ability at Stratford last month but not enough to suggest she'll threaten here

runners.name: Mrs Cravatte
runners.spotlight: Unable to get competitive in points and soundly beaten in a bumper and a maiden hurdle; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Unable to get competitive in points and soundly beaten in a bumper and a maiden hurdle

runners.name: Nannymooresbridge
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in both bumpers and pulled up at 300-1 on hurdle debut last October; a transformation is needed following wind surgery.
runners.comment: Tailed off in both bumpers and pulled up at 300-1 on hurdle debut last October

races.race_name: CopyBet Says Thank You To Hereford Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: I Am Spider Man
runners.spotlight: 9yo who hasn't won since July 2024 but he returned to form when going close at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last Wednesday on his second start for this yard; major player if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Returned to form when going close at Newton Abbot & he's a major player if backing that up

runners.name: Campaign Trail
runners.spotlight: He's returned from a break in good heart this year, finishing fourth at Market Rasen (2m7f, soft) before getting closer to the target when 3l third at Southwell (3m, good); entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Ran well when third at Southwell (3m, good) last time and he's entitled to respect

runners.name: Border Gem
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but she ran well when second here (2m3f, good) in March and rallied for a respectable fifth at Taunton (good) three weeks ago on her first crack at 3m; shaped last time as though today's extra yardage could be in her favour and she might be in the mix.
runners.comment: Rallied at Taunton on first crack at 3m and today's extra yardage could be in her favour

runners.name: Heard That
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner (at up to 3m) who was runner-up over hurdles at Southwell (2m4f, good) in March but he's inconsistent and made it 0-10 over hurdles when tailed off at Perth (2m4f, good) last month; needs to bounce back; non-runner when due to wear these blinkers for the first time 5:47 Southwell Monday.
runners.comment: Second in March but tailed off last month; 0-10 over hurdles; non-runner Southwell Monday

runners.name: Catwalk Girl
runners.spotlight: Fell on handicap hurdle debut at Ludlow last month and this point winner scored at Huntingdon (2m7f, good) last Thursday on her first chase start; she capitalised on the early fall of the favourite last time, and she needs to transfer her form back to hurdles, but she has shown promise in this sphere and races off the same mark as last week; wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won off this mark on chase debut at Huntingdon last week; promise over hurdles previously
runners.owner: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green

races.race_name: First Past The Post At CopyBet Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ikigai Star
runners.spotlight: Largely below par since scoring at Huntingdon (2m4f) last May but he wasn't disgraced when third of five in 2m2f Fontwell handicap last month; needs to build on it now.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when third of five in 2m2f Fontwell h'cap last month; needs to build on it

runners.name: Jack Hoola
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (around 2m4f) at Huntingdon and Southwell late in 2025 but off since a below-par seventh at Doncaster (2m3f) in January when also not looking the easiest of rides; needs to bounce back after a layoff.
runners.comment: Off since below-par Doncaster seventh in January; needs to bounce back after a layoff

runners.name: Ice Jet
runners.spotlight: A fair ex-Irish Flat maiden; brought along steadily over hurdles for current yard before much improved on his handicap debut (on the back of a wind op) when second of 11 at Wetherby (2m) in March, only just failing after a late blunder; major shout despite a 6lb rise.
runners.comment: Much improved on back of a wind op when second at Wetherby latest; up 6lb but major shout

runners.name: Jackeline
runners.spotlight: Course winner in 2024 but she failed to take to fences last season and came in last of seven back in this sphere at Newton Abbot (2m) last month; others are much preferred.
runners.comment: Course winner in 2024; last of seven at Newton Abbot last month; others   much preferred

runners.name: Man Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: In good form without winning (runner-up over 2m3f at Taunton) before Christmas and should be sharper for a recent fifth on the Flat at Bath; this veteran needs considering.
runners.comment: In good form before Christmas; should be sharper for a recent Flat fifth; considered

runners.name: The Padster
runners.spotlight: Only modest form shown thus far and he had breathing surgery before coming in a remote fourth of nine in 2m1f Exeter handicap 25 days ago; cheekpieces are now tried.
runners.comment: Had breathing surgery before remote Exeter fourth 25 days ago; cheekpieces are now tried

runners.name: Midnight Pass
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who returned from six months off with a solid fourth of nine on her handicap debut at Taunton (2m3f) 20 days ago; she can do better off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Solid fourth on h'cap debut at Taunton 20 days ago; she can do better off a 2lb lower mark

runners.name: Celine Man
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 8yo who offered little in a pair of 2m Huntingdon handicaps for his current yard in February; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Offered little in a pair of 2m Huntingdon handicaps for current yard in February

runners.name: Launceston
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden for Sir Mark Prescott; yet to show much for his current yard over hurdles and last of nine back on the Flat at Wolverhampton in February; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Yet to show much for current yard over hurdles and last of nine on Flat in February
runners.owner: S Keable L Cullimore G Tuck

races.race_name: Hereford Season Finale Open National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 1 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: Master Roque
runners.spotlight: £54,000 3yo who contested a valuable bumper at Newbury (2m, good to soft) on his debut last month, finishing 13th of 20 at 22-1 against several previous winners; that gives him something to build on and he could have a big part to play.
runners.comment: Mid-division on debut in valuable bumper at Newbury; could have a big part to play

runners.name: Lost In The Dream
runners.spotlight: Bred to be talented and he defied his evident inexperience to win at 7-2 on last month's debut at Taunton (2m, good), the first two clear; he could have plenty more to offer and holds strong claims under a penalty.
runners.comment: Defied evident inexperience to win on debut at Taunton and could have plenty more to offer

runners.name: Five Lanes
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to three winners; ran out on last month's debut in a British maiden point (2m4f, good to soft); may be best watched on bumper debut.
runners.comment: Ran out on last month's debut in British maiden point; may be best watched on bumper debut

runners.name: Mayo
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability when 11l fourth of six on debut at Stratford (2m, good to firm) last August, then took a backwards step at Worcester (2m, good) in September; has since left Christian Williams and improvement is needed on this first outing for Mickey Bowen; remains in same ownership.
runners.comment: Showed ability on the first of his two starts but improvement necessary on stable debut

races.race_name: RJ King & Sons EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Seed Ya Later
runners.spotlight: Runner-up here before prevailing in a tight finish at Musselburgh (5f, good); solid claims.
runners.comment: Runner-up here before prevailing in a tight finish at Musselburgh (5f, good); solid claims

runners.name: Jolly Good Fellows
runners.spotlight: Lacks scope but it's been a promising start and she battled on well to prevail here last week with the front two clear; won't be far away.
runners.comment: She battled on well to prevail here last week with the front two clear

runners.name: Passerine
runners.spotlight: Behind Seed Ya Later on her debut here and was no better at Salisbury; needs to find something from somewhere.
runners.comment: Behind Seed Ya Later on her debut here and was no better at Salisbury

runners.name: Our Fella
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 1m winner Nituna (RPR 76); dam unraced half-sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Mr Scarlet and Italian 6f 2yo Listed winner Catalina Bay; the only runner without experience.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 1m winner Nituna (RPR 76); the only runner without experience

runners.name: Sovereign Glory
runners.spotlight: Inexperience was evident so it was an encouraging start to be close up here 15 days ago; he was fairly short in the market at 100-30; can improve.
runners.comment: Inexperience was evident so it was an encouraging start to be close up here 15 days ago

runners.name: Devon Angel
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two C&D runs last month; should find nurseries more suitable in time.
runners.comment: Down the field in two C&D runs last month; should find nurseries more suitable in time

runners.name: Super Tuscan
runners.spotlight: 11-1 at Goodwood (5f, good) where he showed early speed before fading; entitled to last longer this time.
runners.comment: 11-1 at Goodwood (5f, good) where he showed early speed before fading

races.race_name: Bath Mind Charity "Confined" Handicap (No Win Since The 2025 Flat Turf Season)

runners.name: The Feminine Urge
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner for previous stable; not far away in two runs over C&D for this one and should be on the premises once more, for all that cheekpieces haven't done much for her when tried twice before.
runners.comment: Three wins for former yard; two solid efforts here for current one; on the premises

runners.name: Hidden Verse
runners.spotlight: Twice a winner in 2025, once here; reappeared with a close sixth at this track last month and it was his first visit to Pontefract when below par three weeks ago; can go well.
runners.comment: If forgiven his Pontefract run (debut there) then it's easy to make a case

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: 3-34 on the AW and 0-9 on turf; has been threatening a breakthrough on grass and looks sure to go well again from the front; blinkers replace usual cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Looks sure to go well again from the front; blinkers replace usual cheekpieces
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: Some Nightmare
runners.spotlight: Eight of his nine wins have been on turf but he was comfortably held back on the grass here two weeks ago.
runners.comment: He was comfortably held back on the grass here two weeks ago
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould & Partner

runners.name: Under Curfew
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is on a lengthy losing run; can lead but didn't at Windsor last time and was never involved; if on his game he wouldn't be without hope.
runners.comment: Can lead but didn't at Windsor last time and was never involved

runners.name: Thurso
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year, at 6f and 7f; needs to get himself back on track and this is only his second attempt at 5f.
runners.comment: Needs to get himself back on track and this is only his second attempt at 5f

runners.name: Danger Alert
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for ages but lurks on a career-low mark and the yard is in form; fourth at Windsor in reapplied cheekpieces on Monday was respectable.
runners.comment: Hasn't won for ages but lurks on a career-low mark; respectable fourth on Monday

runners.name: Dapperling
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 2-30 and her habit of starting slowly appears to be going nowhere; opposable.
runners.comment: Strike-rate of 2-30 and habit of starting slowly appears to be going nowhere; opposable

races.race_name: Best Cleaning Group Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Noble Vow
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and has pulled too hard in both his races this season, over C&D and 6f on the AW; the ability is there if he gives himself a chance.
runners.comment: 0-6 and has pulled too hard in both his races this season, over C&D and 6f on the AW

runners.name: Queen Sana
runners.spotlight: Only headed late on in a Windsor 5f handicap last week and these terms are by no means impossible to overcome back in a maiden.
runners.comment: Only headed late on in a Windsor 5f handicap last week

runners.name: In The City
runners.spotlight: Beaten five times but competitive in all four races for this yard and this looks his trip; solid chance for all that he has to concede weight to younger opposition.
runners.comment: Beaten five times but competitive in all four runs for this yard and this looks his trip

runners.name: Far From Fern
runners.spotlight: First foal out of a 5.7f winner (RPR 56) and half-sister to 5.7f/6f winner Titus Secret; gelded ahead of debut and best watched.
runners.comment: First foal out of a 5.7f winner (RPR 56) and half-sister to 5.7f/6f winner Titus Secret

runners.name: Princess Of Limnos
runners.spotlight: Showed very little in two runs for her previous yard, over 6f on the AW.
runners.comment: Showed very little in two runs for her previous yard, over 6f on the AW

races.race_name: Pockets At Bath Handicap

runners.name: Raintown
runners.spotlight: Exposed multiple winner who could improve for having a recent run here under his belt, with that his first appearance in three months; handicapped to win again.
runners.comment: Handicapped to win again and his recent third here was after three months off
runners.owner: Ms Caroline Instone & J S Moore

runners.name: Electric Bass
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated at Kempton in November but was subsequently demoted; now 0-12 but ran a pleasing race in defeat at Southwell last time after 130 days away; races prominently.
runners.comment: Ran a pleasing race in defeat at Southwell last time after 130 days away

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: Often goes well here, as again was the case when an 80-1 runner-up last week (good); likely to be ridden handy as usual.
runners.comment: Often goes well here, as again was the case when an 80-1 runner-up last week

runners.name: Hibernate
runners.spotlight: Dictated matters from the front to win in a small field at Yarmouth 12 days ago (1m1f, good to firm); effectively 9lb higher as they claimed 5lb that day, but stamina won't be an issue over this far.
runners.comment: Dictated matters from the front to win in a small field at Yarmouth; effectively up 9lb

races.race_name: Clyde And Co Handicap

runners.name: Criminal
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who returned to form with a near-miss over 1m2f on the AW 14 days ago, despite being slow to break; first-time headgear might do something for him; effective on grass.
runners.comment: Nearly won on the AW latest despite starting slowly; cheekpieces could work

runners.name: Galaxy Wonder
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick after wins at Wolverhampton (8.6f) and here (1m, good to soft); raised 7lb for last time but that was fairly comfortable and he's likeably consistent; stays this far.
runners.comment: Raised 7lb for last time but that was fairly comfortable and he's likeably consistent

runners.name: Party Island
runners.spotlight: Most wins on the AW but it was his third success on the grass when coming home 2.5l to the good over C&D two weeks ago; defying the 5lb rise shouldn't be beyond him.
runners.comment: Third success on the grass when coming home 2.5l to the good over C&D two weeks ago

runners.name: Premier
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery since a third over hurdles in February when last seen; his only Flat win came here on soft ground and seems to handle any conditions; mark is realistic.
runners.comment: Had wind op since last run over hurdles; sole Flat win came at this track

runners.name: Prodigal Son
runners.spotlight: Travelled well in the new blinkers over 1m here recently (firm) and got the job done by just over a length; has stamina to prove over this longer trip and no guarantees the headgear will work a second time.
runners.comment: In first-time blinkers when winning here over 1m; stamina to prove over this far

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Now Handicap

runners.name: My Ambition
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who has returned from a break with two competitive shows, the latest here; hard to knock.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner who has returned from a break with two competitive shows

runners.name: Thapa Vc
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner; goes well at this track and was outstayed over 1m2f here 15 days ago; this trip is a better fit for him.
runners.comment: Outstayed over 1m2f here 15 days ago; this trip is a better fit for him

runners.name: Rovinia
runners.spotlight: Mainly seen on the AW (1-8) and hasn't quite produced the same figures on turf; however, still early days and her only previous try at 1m was too bad to be true; others are probably safer, however.
runners.comment: 1-12 and better on the AW; has only raced at 1m once before; others look safer

runners.name: Kondratiev Wave
runners.spotlight: Eight career wins divided between AW and turf, and he's won from 5f to 1m; returns to turf on the back of a string of pleasing AW runs and no reason why he won't have a say; races prominently.
runners.comment: Returns to turf on the back of a string of pleasing AW runs

runners.name: Purple Sky
runners.spotlight: A number of modest efforts since an Irish win over further last summer; others safer.
runners.comment: A number of modest efforts since an Irish win over further last summer; others safer

runners.name: Vecchio
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who hasn't offered much for a good while now.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden who hasn't offered much for a good while now

runners.name: Nammos
runners.spotlight: Debut for a seventh different yard having been beaten at short prices three times for James Owen; might be better over shorter.
runners.comment: Different yard having been beaten at short prices three times for James Owen

runners.name: Racing Demon
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been over 1m, twice here; has been running well over further since his last win but slow starts have become a habit.
runners.comment: Capable handicapper and goes well here, but slow starts have become a habit

runners.name: Lady Of The Isles
runners.spotlight: No wins in seven; has run well in a couple of handicaps (1m/1m2f) but she finished last tried in blinkers last time and those aids are retained.
runners.comment: She finished last tried in blinkers last time and those aids are retained

runners.name: Havana Club
runners.spotlight: Got behind after a slow start here last time but still got to within a head of the winner; the hood is removed but one to consider.
runners.comment: Got behind after a slow start here last time but still got to within a head of the winner

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Alphonsus Liguori
runners.spotlight: Cost 210,000gns as a yearling; Study Of Man colt was beaten only a neck by shorter-priced stablemate Perisher on debut over 1m on soft ground at Gowran; finished 4l clear of an experienced 79-rated rival; can score with normal improvement
runners.comment: Beaten a neck by a shorter-priced stablemate on debut, can score with normal improvement

runners.name: Dark Leader
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel gelding; 65,000gns 2yo Breeze Up purchase; unraced last year; showed plenty of promise when second over 7f at Limerick last month, should go close.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of promise when second over 7f at Limerick last month, should go close

runners.name: Mergus Serrator
runners.spotlight: Respectable run at Leopardstown in October on only outing at two; shaped quite well when keeping on to take fourth in a 1m2f maiden at Navan; might not be as effective at this trip.
runners.comment: Kept on to take fourth in a 1m2f maiden at Navan, might not be as effective at this trip.

runners.name: Chamonix
runners.spotlight: E100,000 yearling; weak in the betting on debut (20-1) when fifth of eight at the Curragh early last month (1m1f, soft to heavy); faded into fourth of eight in a 1m Cork maiden three weeks later; stable has a stronger contender in Alphonsus Liguori.
runners.comment: Mild promise in two starts, stable has a stronger contender in Alphonsus Liguori

runners.name: Oro Blanco
runners.spotlight: E36,000 2yo Breeze Up; can be expected to improve from Limerick debut; needs to reverse form with runner-up Dark Leader.
runners.comment: Can be expected to improve from Limerick debut, needs to reverse form with Dark Leader

runners.name: Henry Hudson
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 1m winner, half-sister to winners Little Chesney (US 5f stakes) and Forever Thing (US stakes-placed).
runners.comment: 30,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 1m winner, half-sister to two winners in the US

runners.name: Vico Road
runners.spotlight: E6,000 Y; third foal; half-brother to 5f/6f winner Dandy Magic; dam 7f-1m4f AW winner, half-sister to 1m Listed winner Barys; stable does well at this venue.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 5f/6f winner Dandy Magic; dam an AW winner, stable does well at this venue

runners.name: Amrum
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Irish 2,000 Guineas winner Siskin; nondescript run on debut at Dundalk; never a threat over a longer trip on soft at Cork; not an obvious contender at this stage.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Irish 2,000 Guineas winner Siskin, no sign of winning potential so far

runners.name: Res Ipsa
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt is a brother to his trainer's smart Group 3 winner Red Letter; well fancied on debut at Leopardstown last August; looked raw and met trouble in running, could be a different proposition now; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Looked raw and met trouble in running on debut, could be a different proposition now

runners.name: Lonely Island
runners.spotlight: Failed to make much impact on debut at Dundalk last December when second-favourite for a 1m maiden dominated by outsiders.
runners.comment: Failed to make much impact on debut at Dundalk last December when second-favourite

runners.name: Second Line
runners.spotlight: Slow to break and never a threat on Fairyhouse debut last June, an excuse emerged then; hard to make a case for him based on a Dundalk run in January.
runners.comment: Excuse for a poor run at two, hard to make a case for him based on a Dundalk outing

runners.name: Connor Of Mali
runners.spotlight: Big price at Thurles on only start and ran accordingly; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Big price at Thurles on only start and ran accordingly, safe to rule out

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Race

runners.name: Glyfada
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to a twice-raced rival on debut at the Curragh, over 1m1f on very testing ground; improved on that when stepped up in trip on better ground at Naas; shorter possibly not ideal for this Camelot filly, entered in the Pretty Polly and Irish Oaks; however, she is probably the form horse.
runners.comment: Winner over 1m2f, shorter perhaps not ideal but fair chance she can cope in this grade

runners.name: Flawless Fusion
runners.spotlight: Second in 7f Leopardstown fillies' maiden (soft) last June on only start for Jim Bolger; following month bought for 140,000gns; failed to run to expectations at Dundalk on yard debut but split a Ballydoyle pair at Galway in October; earned a mark of 76 that is unlikely to be good enough to account for previous winners.
runners.comment: Earned a mark of 76 that is unlikely to be good enough to account for previous winners

runners.name: What About Us
runners.spotlight: E160,000 Tattersalls Ireland Breeze Up purchase last May; made an encouraging debut when second in a 7f maiden at Dundalk last October; failed to improve when third at the same venue on November; stable in terrific form, champion jockey aboard.
runners.comment: Second in a 7f maiden at Dundalk on debut, failed to show improvement on her second AW run

runners.name: Mighty Danu
runners.spotlight: Looked raw first time at Galway and did well to get the better of the twice-raced runner-up; set a tough task in a Listed race at Leopardstown won by the brilliant Diamond Necklace; tongue-tie added for reappearance.
runners.comment: Debut winner, then set a tough task in a Listed race won by the brilliant Diamond Necklace

runners.name: Sursum Corda
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced despite finishing 7 last of six at Leopardstown (sent off 150-1) in a good 7f maiden at Leopardstown last May on only juvenile start; another respectable run at Cork on reappearance; possibly capable of winning races but may need her sights lowered.
runners.comment: Two fair runs, possibly capable of winning races but may need her sights lowered

runners.name: Mo Mhuirin
runners.spotlight: Long-priced winner of a 1m maiden at Dundalk last December; can continue to pay her way for a stable well on its way to a best-ever year.
runners.comment: Long-priced winner of a 1m maiden at Dundalk last December, can continue to pay her way

races.race_name: FEXCO Maiden

runners.name: Treasure Rose
runners.spotlight: Now a four-time runner-up; not exactly progressive but might get a change of fortune now.
runners.comment: Now a four-time runner-up; not exactly progressive but might get a change of fortune now

runners.name: Mystic Bella
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame but does not seem entirely reliable; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Has made the frame but does not seem entirely reliable; others have stronger claims

runners.name: Tyraxes
runners.spotlight: First seven runs were on AW; placed in three of five Dundalk outings for this stable, including a 3l third over this trip at Dundalk in March; made little impression at Limerick on turf debut; better ground may help.
runners.comment: Fair placed form on AW, made little impression on turf debut, better ground may help.

runners.name: Coul Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Won a juvenile hurdle by 9l at Punchestown in November 2024; has run well in defeat on the Flat, placed three times, including second at Clonmel last autumn; well below best on seasonal return in a 1m4f handicap at the Curragh; seems most effective at around 1m2f.
runners.comment: Won a juvenile hurdle in 2024, fair Flat form, poor display in a 1m4f handicap in March
runners.owner: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings

runners.name: Hygge
runners.spotlight: Third in both starts in academy hurdles (beaten favourite both times); same position in 4yo maidens at Navan and Cork, beaten over 20l both times; half-brother to two Flat winners; dam, a Group-placed 1m winner by Galileo, half-sister Giants Causeway; of some interest on Flat debut.
runners.comment: 115-rated maiden hurdler, has a good Flat pedigree, of some interest on Flat debut

runners.name: Dazzling Angel
runners.spotlight: Trained by Paddy Twomey at two; promising fourth over 7f at Gowran on debut; failed to justify short-priced favouritism at Dundalk; winner Ms Willpower won her only start at three; might need the run but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Showed winning potential at two, unraced last season, might need the run now

runners.name: Teffian Warrior
runners.spotlight: Best effort from five maidens came at Galway last July when a 6l fifth of 14 (7f, yielding); led before fading into fourth of eighth on handicap/seasonal debut at Leopardstown last month (1m1f, yielding to soft); likely capable of better; cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Led before fading into fourth at Leopardstown, likely capable of better, cheekpieces now

runners.name: The Rock Factor
runners.spotlight: Ran in a Naas bumper in January; mid-field in Flat maidens at Navan and Cork; more likely one for handicaps in time.
runners.comment: Mid-field in Flat maidens at Navan and Cork, more likely one for handicaps in time

runners.name: Phidias Ovation
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens at Naas (1m, yielding) and Dundalk (7f) last year; no better at Limerick (1m, yielding) on reappearance.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in maidens at Naas and Dundalk last year, no better at Limerick last month

runners.name: Taj Crown
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and ran green at Dundalk on her only start at three; visored now,; stable had a maiden winner at Ballinrobe last Friday; best watched.
runners.comment: Slowly away and ran green at Dundalk on her only start at three, visored now, best watched

runners.name: Radient Man
runners.spotlight: Down in a 6f maiden at the Curragh last June; looked an awkward ride when around 10l fifth of eight over C&D last October.
runners.comment: Looked an awkward ride when around 10l fifth of eight over C&D last October

runners.name: Crosshaven
runners.spotlight: Dropped away from two furlongs out on debut over 1m2f at the Curragh last May; not seen again after a moderate run over 1m5f at Leopardstown in June; gelded now; jockey booking is the only positive.
runners.comment: Two moderate runs over further last season, jockey booking is the only positive.

runners.name: Tijuana
runners.spotlight: Low-rated gelding, unplaced in ten outings between 2021 and 2023; huge task after long absence.
runners.comment: Low-rated gelding, unplaced in ten outings between 2021 and 2023, huge task after layoff

races.race_name: Velo Coffee Roasters Handicap

runners.name: Jasmine Affanalis
runners.spotlight: Won last season off only 2lb lower and was only 3l away when fifth of 18 on her return to turf at Cork (1m, good to yielding); each-way claims, non-runner on Monday at Roscommon (5.23).
runners.comment: Less to prove than a good few of these, non-runner on Monday at Roscommon (5.23)

runners.name: Lorr's Girl
runners.spotlight: Chance on last summer's form but absent since below par during the winter and she's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Chance on last summer's form but absent since below par during the winter and drawn wide

runners.name: Nibras Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner but needed to run better on his recent stable debut to be seriously considered.
runners.comment: Four-time winner but needed to run better on recent yard debut to be seriously considered

runners.name: Swiss Army Officer
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D (soft) in October but this veteran was down the field at Cork on last month's reappearance.
runners.comment: Ran well here in October but was down the field at Cork on last month's reappearance

runners.name: Friars Gate
runners.spotlight: Best maiden effort was her latest when ninth of 15 at Limerick (1m, yielding) and brings some potential into handicaps.
runners.comment: Best maiden effort was her latest when ninth of 15 at Limerick; handicap debut

runners.name: Hot To Foxtrot
runners.spotlight: Competitive in 7f/6f handicaps on the AW this season and she has form on turf; does have stamina to prove over this far but interesting otherwise; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Competitive in 7f/6f handicaps on the AW this season and she has form on turf

runners.name: Hale Bopp
runners.spotlight: Reserve; stuck on a long losing run but continues to go well now and again, as on his penultimate start.
runners.comment: Reserve; stuck on a long losing run but continues to go well now and again

runners.name: Saith Seren
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; below her best in two runs after an absence but she's run well here before and lurks on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden but she's run well here before and lurks on a dangerous mark

runners.name: Tero d'Rosa
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Ballinrobe four days ago when he dug deep for a narrow success at the expense of the odds-on Majestic King who beat Can I Kiss You the run before; doesn't look obviously well in under a penalty and stall 14 isn't ideal, but must have a chance.
runners.comment: Dug deep to win four days ago; not obviously well in under a penalty but respected

runners.name: Mogwli
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner but this season hasn't started at all well and best watched for now.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner but this season hasn't started at all well and best watched for now

runners.name: Peig Sayers
runners.spotlight: Only raced fives times but has finished down the field in both her handicaps (1m); enough to prove.
runners.comment: Only raced fives times but has finished down the field in both her handicaps (1m)

runners.name: Chipsrdown
runners.spotlight: Signs of progress on handicap debut at Leopardstown (7f) where she was never closer than in the finish; can probably improve again if overcoming her wide stall.
runners.comment: Signs of progress on handicap debut at Leopardstown when never closer than at the finish

runners.name: Toy Soldier
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden and a claimer in Britain; no worthwhile form in seven runs for current yard.
runners.comment: Won a maiden and a claimer in Britain; no worthwhile form in seven runs for current yard

runners.name: Can I Kiss You
runners.spotlight: Her first placing when bumping into one ahead of its mark last week at Gowran (1m, good to yielding); something similar will see her bang there.
runners.comment: Her first placing when bumping into one ahead of its mark last week at Gowran

runners.name: Secret Magician
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 3-50; couldn't draw on any positives from his four runs this year but getting back on drier ground should help matters.
runners.comment: Exposed; has been struggling in the mud; better can be expected on this surface

runners.name: Ethelwulf
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ability for the Gosdens but very opposable on this year's form; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Reserve; ability for the Gosdens but very opposable on this year's form

runners.name: Tynamite
runners.spotlight: Veteran; six AW wins but is 0-32 on turf and doesn't arrive here in the best of form.
runners.comment: Veteran; six AW wins but is 0-32 on turf and doesn't arrive here in the best of form

runners.name: Morning Approach
runners.spotlight: Reserve; struggled through the winter at Dundalk but showed more when fifth at Roscommon on Monday.
runners.comment: Reserve; signs of returning to form when fifth at Roscommon on Monday

races.race_name: Brehon Hotel Handicap

runners.name: Celestially
runners.spotlight: Navan fourth on handicap debut was a step in the right direction, though beaten 9l; more needed; one of four for the stable.
runners.comment: Navan fourth on handicap debut was a step in the right direction, beaten 9l, more needed

runners.name: Coolangatta
runners.spotlight: Midfield at Cork on her second start at two; hard to take encouragement from her seasonal debut; one of four runners for the stable.
runners.comment: Midfield at Cork on her second start, hard to take encouragement from her seasonal debut

runners.name: Chicago Moon
runners.spotlight: Has a clear chance based on her second Dundalk run, when third behind two colts; two other runs raise doubts.
runners.comment: Has a clear chance based on her second Dundalk run, two other much lesser displays
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; more exposed than several of these rivals and may remain vulnerable; ridden by a 10lb claimer on her last three starts; Shane Foley booked this time; has a bit to find with Celestially on running at Navan, when Foley partnered her.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden, more exposed than several of these rivals and may remain vulnerable

runners.name: Delusional
runners.spotlight: Has failed to make much impact on his first two handicap attempts; needs to raise his game; first-time visor.
runners.comment: Has failed to make much impact on his first two handicap attempts, needs to raise his game

runners.name: Blissful Bonita
runners.spotlight: Debut on turf was better than two subsequent turf outings; worth considering in this grade on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Debut on turf was better than two subsequent turf outings, worth considering

runners.name: Divine Believer
runners.spotlight: Seventh of 13 in her first handicap and trailed home last at Leopardstown on Sunday.
runners.comment: Seventh of 13 in her first handicap and trailed home last at Leopardstown on Sunday

runners.name: Mount Eden
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two runs on AW; hard to fancy after a lifeless Gowran run; cheekpieces now.
runners.comment: Modest form in two runs on AW, hard to fancy after a lifeless Gowran run, cheekpieces now
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Musashi
runners.spotlight: Beat just a total of two horses in his maidens last season, at 1m, 7f and 6f; however, as a step up in trip made a difference at Dundalk last month when he took second over the extended 1m2f, with Cameclose back in fourth; yard in superb form.
runners.comment: Weak form at 1m and below at two, step up in trip made a difference at Dundalk last month

runners.name: Honouramongthieves
runners.spotlight: Pick of three juvenile efforts when a staying-on second over C&D in testing conditions; well beaten on handicap debut in a 1m4f Cork contest.
runners.comment: Best run at was over 1m at this venue in testing conditions, well beaten on handicap debut

runners.name: Gatlinburg
runners.spotlight: Has shown only modest ability in maidens, this longer trip may help to produce improvement; possibly the pick of four runners for   his trainer; the market may confirm if that is the case or not.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest ability in maidens, this longer trip may help to produce improvement
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Cameclose
runners.spotlight: Fourth of six at Dundalk in his first handicap, has a bit to find with runner-up Musashi; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Fourth of six at Dundalk in his first handicap, has a bit to find with runner-up Musashi

races.race_name: Outlook Publications (Q.R.) Handicap

runners.name: Wandering Rocks
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner; seemingly perked up by the new blinkers when a rallying third at Dundalk 19 days ago (1m4f); whether he'll be as good again is open to some debate.
runners.comment: Seemingly perked up by the new blinkers when a rallying third at Dundalk 19 days ago

runners.name: Factual Fact
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who was closing on the winner in a 1m4f handicap at the Curragh in March; 4lb higher but appeals as a horse with more to offer; all his form is on slow ground, so that's the one concern.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who was closing on the winner in a 1m4f handicap at the Curragh in March

runners.name: Dark Note
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner who has a win apiece over hurdles and fences; well beaten over hurdles in March on his return from a break but ran some solid Flat races last year, including over C&D.
runners.comment: Has been jumping; ran some solid Flat races last year, including over C&D

runners.name: Hidalgo Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Signed off last year with a poor run on the AW and was beaten 22l on his reappearance over hurdles; hasn't had many chances on the Flat but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden; hasn't had many chances on the Flat but others appeal more

runners.name: Slurricane
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler who has won on the Flat and ran respectably last month at Gowran in a 1m6f handicap; unlikely to be far away.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler who has won on the Flat and ran respectably last month at Gowran

runners.name: Riyami
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles but all four Flat wins have been at Dundalk; since that last win in April he's finished well beaten on the AW and on chase debut.
runners.comment: Has won over hurdles but all four Flat wins have been at Dundalk

runners.name: Harry's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Dual purpose performer who was nicely ahead of Slurricane when a close third at Gowran a month ago (1m6f, soft); player.
runners.comment: Nicely ahead of Slurricane when a close third at Gowran a month ago

runners.name: Sequoiaspirit
runners.spotlight: Strong stayer with the form to feature off this mark but he has been very quiet in two runs over hurdles this year.
runners.comment: Form to feature but he has been very quiet in two runs over hurdles this year

runners.name: Winning Smut
runners.spotlight: No closer than 14l in three Flat maidens (for Michael Hourigan) and better known as a jumper, having won a Listed handicap hurdle at last year's Galway festival; could be dangerous off this low a mark.
runners.comment: Useful winning hurdler; unexposed on the Flat and lurks on a dangerous mark

runners.name: Chain Of Destiny
runners.spotlight: Placed in a Curragh maiden back in 2022; that's his only piece of notable form and he's returning from a mammoth absence.
runners.comment: One piece of form came in a maiden; returning from a mammoth absence

races.race_name: Killarney Racegoers Club Race

runners.name: Defiantly
runners.spotlight: Highly tried at Listed level last season after winning his maiden here; the concern is a very laboured comeback run on handicap debut at Cork.
runners.comment: The concern is a very laboured comeback run on handicap debut at Cork

runners.name: Sky Captain
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 1m4f at Roscommon and held his own against smart opposition over 1m6f at Galway; still unexposed and canny trainer fits a visor.
runners.comment: Made a very solid start to career last season and he's far from exposed

runners.name: Tim Toe
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner (on good) who added a 1m3f maiden at Bellewstown last month (soft); he'll need to better that form if he's to be winning this.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who added a 1m3f maiden at Bellewstown last month; more needed

runners.name: Kizlyar
runners.spotlight: French winner at this trip; ran okay in three maiden hurdles for this yard but was tailed off at Navan on Flat handicap debut, albeit on heavy ground; his French win was on good to soft, so that heightens interest for today.
runners.comment: Flopped on heavy last time; his French win was on good to soft; not yet exposed

runners.name: Farren Glory
runners.spotlight: Grade 1-winning novice hurdler and has also won over fences; hasn't reached the heights he once promised over jumps but he wasn't disgraced in a strong Grade 2 at Fairyhouse last month; not entirely dismissed.
runners.comment: He wasn't disgraced in a strong Grade 2 hurdle at Fairyhouse last month

runners.name: Ebony King
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; close second in a 1m4f AW maiden before filling the same berth in a Grade 2 novice hurdle at Fairyhouse last month; good chance here.
runners.comment: Developing into a smart hurdler and runner-up on the AW on sole Flat start

runners.name: Stellar Quality
runners.spotlight: Third here on her debut last summer and then won over 1m4f at Bellewstown; well bred, in top hands and retains plenty of potential.
runners.comment: Third here on her debut last summer and then won over 1m4f at Bellewstown

races.race_name: Adare Manor Opportunity Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Lilac Lady
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden; all four runs over hurdles came at her local track last year; third in maiden and in a handicap off 92; will be better for this comeback run.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden; four runs over hurdles here last year; third in maiden and a handicap

runners.name: Hey Now
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in a decent bumper and 7-1, creditable fifth on hurdling debut at Clonmel, third won since, fourth now rated 107; latest run was on soft and he's bred for good ground, although this track might be tight enough for good-sized gelding.
runners.comment: 7-1, creditable fifth on hurdling debut at Clonmel, third won since, fourth now rated 107

runners.name: Saint Peregrine
runners.spotlight: Only rated 91, but second in this last year and again back here in June; lost form after and has been off for 173 days; needs to revive.
runners.comment: Only rated 91, but second in this last year and again back here in June; lost form after

runners.name: Tawny Lea
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to smart 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Duke Of Navan; dam well beaten in bumpers, sister to point winner Abbeyknock Boy, half-sister to 2m4f/2m6f chase winner Big Al.
runners.comment: Half-sister to smart 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Duke Of Navan; best watched on debut

runners.name: Ludus Magnus
runners.spotlight: First two bumper runs were decent; 11-2, tailed off last time over 2m3f; hurdling debut.
runners.comment: First two bumper runs were decent; 11-2, tailed off last time over 2m3f; hurdling debut

runners.name: Strategic Move
runners.spotlight: E15,000 3yo; fifth foal; half-brother to bumper winner Code Name Lise; dam unraced sister to useful 2m1f/2m3f hurdle winner Captain Kirkton, half-sister to useful winners Klepht (2m3f-2m6f hurdle/chase) and Welsh Shadow (bumper and 2m/2m4f hurdle); pulled up in a point in November.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a bumper winner was pulled up in a point in November

runners.name: Game Point
runners.spotlight: Useful but frustrating for Ger Lyons on the Flat, runner-up five times in a row in maidens; only okay runs in two recent starts for this yard and mark now down to 78; tried in tongue-tie latest, now hooded for hurdling debut
runners.comment: Runner-up five times in a row in Flat maidens for Ger Lyons; hood on for hurdling debut

runners.name: First Ascent
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Godolphin cast-off had some good Flat form (rated 77) for Matt Smith; pulled up lame on stable/hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Had some good Flat form (rated 77) for Matt Smith; pulled up lame on stable/hurdle debut

runners.name: William F Browne
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f/1m at two for Ger Lyons; nearly won Galway handicap off 65 last summer; hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Won over 7f/1m at two and nearly won Galway handicap off 65 last summer; hurdling debut

runners.name: Denham Rock
runners.spotlight: Homebred Mahler gelding out of hurdles winner; 28-1 when tailed off in Punchstown bumper.
runners.comment: Homebred Mahler gelding out of hurdles winner; 28-1 when tailed off in Punchstown bumper

runners.name: Casual Water
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to top-class Altior and several other smart performers; maybe telling that last month's debut was over 3m at Limerick where he was tailed off on bad ground; over a shorter trip and on drier terrain now, we'll see if there's anything there.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Altior; tailed off on last month's debut over 3m

runners.name: Dahab
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three Flat maidens for Noel Kelly and rated 70; beaten 20l at Ayr on hurdling bow; new trainer fits a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three Flat maidens and rated 70; beaten 20l at Ayr on hurdling bow; new yard

runners.name: Finns First
runners.spotlight: Huge price for Bellewstown maiden, not that fluent before making late headway (beaten 36l).
runners.comment: Huge price for Bellewstown maiden, not fluent before making late headway (beaten 36l)

runners.name: Atlantic Gamble
runners.spotlight: Six-time Flat winner in Britain; not shown much yet hurdling and has to concede weight.
runners.comment: Six-time Flat winner in Britain; not shown much yet hurdling and has to concede weight .

runners.name: Screaming Eagle
runners.spotlight: Regressed on the Flat in Britain (rated 70 now); unfancied when twice well held over hurdles a year ago.
runners.comment: Regressed on the Flat in Britain; unfancied when twice well held over hurdles a year ago

runners.name: Antoine De Paris
runners.spotlight: Won Flat claimer here last summer; huge prices when not involved in two hurdle runs and been off five months.
runners.comment: Won Flat claimer here last summer; not involved in two hurdle runs and off five months

races.race_name: DecoClip Construction Company Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Beir Bua
runners.spotlight: In two runs last August, she won a Wexford bumper and was second on hurdling debut at Kilbeggan; tongue-tie worn in both of those races is now left off.
runners.comment: Bumper winner was second on hurdling debut at Kilbeggan in August; off since

runners.name: Lumiere Du Large
runners.spotlight: Flat winner and second over hurdles in France; cut no ice in Grade 1s at huge prices in early 2025 and regressed alarmingly in three runs last season; makes a stable switch that doesn't happen very often, from Willie Mullins to Gordon Elliott.
runners.comment: Went the wrong way last season and has switched from Willie Mullins to Gordon Elliott

runners.name: Louie's Folly
runners.spotlight: Has placed form over 2m/2m1f on the level; favourite when runner-up in Limerick maiden hurdle in March; 2m5f was expected to suit last time, but it didn't; gets plenty of weight from her main rivals.
runners.comment: Favourite when runner-up in Limerick maiden hurdle in March; 2m5f didn't help last time

runners.name: Keilah
runners.spotlight: Not a bad handicapper at around 1m1f/1m2f for Willie McCreery and fair form in three spins over hurdles, although tended to be too keen; picked up for 5,000euros in November; had a recent comeback run on the Flat.
runners.comment: Flat winner rated 104 after three starts over hurdles; had a warm-up on the level here

runners.name: Brosna Queen
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on good to yielding last summer, not up to Graded level afterwards; wasn't right when pulled up on hurdling debut; lost ground at the start at Fairyhouse last time and ran okay to a point in a decent contest.
runners.comment: Bumper winner lost ground at the start at Fairyhouse last time, shaped okay in sixth

runners.name: Oriole
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent on the Flat but useful on her day and three wins to her name, including over 1m4f at 2024 Galway festival; hurdling debut and has been off since November.
runners.comment: Three-time winner on the Flat (rated 73); sire gets jumps winners, so of some interest

runners.name: Colugo
runners.spotlight: Made the frame a good few times at Dundalk off marks in the 50s; back from a break for hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Made the frame a good few times at Dundalk last year off marks in the 50s; hurdling debut

runners.name: Leva Deva
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, half-sister to 1m2f winner Vinces, out of 1m2f winner
runners.comment: Idaho 5yo; dam 2m hurdle winner, half-sister to 1m2f winner Vinces, out of 1m2f winner

runners.name: Shegotrhythmtspare
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Regina Dracones (2m-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Calvic (point/2m4f chase); dam pulled up in point, half-sister to bumper/smart 2m-3m hurdle winner Alpha Ridge.
runners.comment: Half-sister to useful mare Regina Dracones (2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner)

runners.name: Dont Break My Word
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to point winner Ollies Gal, out of maiden half-sister to bumper/2m5f hurdle winner It's A Discovery.
runners.comment: First foal of 2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner; best watched

runners.name: Circus Hill
runners.spotlight: Second runner from local yard; decent bumper form last year, caught late over a bit farther here and then beaten 5l at Tipperary in October; off since; half-sister to a winning hurdler.
runners.comment: Decent bumper form; caught late over a bit farther here and then beaten 5l at Tipperary
runners.owner: Dermot J Kelly & E J Burns & Sandra C Kelly

runners.name: Tullyegan Jet
runners.spotlight: 200-1 when pulled up in a bumper at Navan; hood left off.
runners.comment: 200-1 when pulled up in a bumper at Navan; hood left off.

runners.name: Crystabel
runners.spotlight: Second in a bumper for Andrew Balding; wasn't going to be beaten far when a late faller at Killarney, but tailed off here when last seen in October.
runners.comment: Wasn't going to be beaten far when a late faller at Killarney, tailed off here in October
runners.owner: M R D Fleming & G Bruce & J P Ryan & Miss G Ryan

runners.name: Cola Blaze
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner early in her career and has been overfaced several times since in both bumpers and over hurdles; this is more realistic but she was beaten 15l in a handicap last time; tried in a combination of headgear.
runners.comment: Bumper winner is rated 100 over hurdles; beaten 15l in handicap last time; headgear combo

runners.name: Urinmydreams
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when tailed off in a bumper here last summer.
runners.comment: 33-1 when tailed off in a bumper here last summer.

runners.name: Chisel Hill
runners.spotlight: Huge price but showed some promise when beaten 33l at Punchestown in February; drops back a couple of furlongs on better ground.
runners.comment: Huge price but showed some promise when beaten 33l at Punchestown in February

runners.name: Annalough
runners.spotlight: Reserve; local mare rated 72; just out for a run in this maiden.
runners.comment: Local mare rated 72; just out for a run in this maiden; reserve

races.race_name: Rosses Point Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bowgate Street
runners.spotlight: First past the post here in 2024 (subsequently disqualified due to the discovery of a prohibited substance); had a good run at Kilbeggan last time on her second run back from a winter break
runners.comment: First past the post here in 2024 and had a good run at Kilbeggan last time

runners.name: Sidiriya
runners.spotlight: Not far off runner-up Classy CC when third at Naas in March, but that is her only placed effort from 16 starts over hurdles; well behind Bowgate Street at Kilbeggan last time.
runners.comment: Third at Naas in March, but that is her only placed effort from 16 starts over hurdles

runners.name: Classy Cc
runners.spotlight: Has a chance on the form of her second at Naas in March, her second placed effort on testing ground; possibly 2m5f was too far when running poorly last time.
runners.comment: Has a chance on the form of her second at Naas in March; possibly trip was too far latest

runners.name: Centaq
runners.spotlight: Yard is back in form and 6yo has been second three times, including at Kilbeggan latest when no match for gambled-on winner but clear of the rest; likes good ground and now tried in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Yard is back in form and 6yo has been second three times, including at Kilbeggan latest

runners.name: Bangor Lady
runners.spotlight: From a family that this trainer has done well with; was running well in Clonmel maiden when coming down two out; that was back in December, but not ruled out on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Was running well in Clonmel maiden when coming down two out; not ruled out on handicap bow

runners.name: Bruno From Mars
runners.spotlight: Maiden made the frame twice over 2m during the winter; longer trips may not have suited since.
runners.comment: Made the frame twice over 2m in the winter; longer trips may not have suited since

runners.name: Lakefield Flyer
runners.spotlight: Hsn't run over hurdles since late 2024; after nine months off, gained second Downpatrick chase win last July, so latest layoff not a concern and he's 15lb lower in this sphere.
runners.comment: Is rated 15lb higher over fences, so has to be considered despite a seven month layoff

runners.name: Moulane West
runners.spotlight: Gained his only hurdles win in this race a year ago, making all to win easily on good ground after 200 days off; trying to repeat that feat; also ran well here a couple of times in August before form tailed off.
runners.comment: Well exposed and lacks a recent run; local hope probably best watched this time

runners.name: Jr Mahon
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; rated 67 on the Flat; some fair runs in maiden hurdles and may have needed latest when fifth at Bellewstown; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Rated 67 on the Flat; some fair runs in maiden hurdles and may have needed latest; reserve

runners.name: Grace's Legacy
runners.spotlight: 20-1 for handicap debut at Clonmel when beaten 29l; drier ground and headgear may help; stable is in decent form and has good record at Sligo.
runners.comment: 20-1 for handicap debut at Clonmel when beaten 29l; drier ground and headgear may help

runners.name: Redcliff Glen
runners.spotlight: First reserve; good-ground handicap winner in 2024; largely out of form since and lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Good-ground handicap winner in 2024; largely out of form since; reserve

runners.name: Brave Josee
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly hasn't shaped too badly in maidens and represents very capable trainer; nice French pedigree and no surprise if she can be competitive in handicaps; watch the market.
runners.comment: 4yo filly hasn't shaped too badly in maidens and represents very capable trainer

runners.name: Go Avel
runners.spotlight: Little encouragement to be derived from the formbook for this mare, and she's been off a year.
runners.comment: Little to encourage in the formbook for this mare and she's been off a year
runners.owner: Mrs Peter Casey & Mrs Paula Casey

runners.name: Kentucky Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Second handicap start saw her beaten 13l at Cork when sixth of 18; that was back in August, but top yard and not out of it.
runners.comment: Showed more when sixth in Cork handicap, but that was back in August

runners.name: Gold Gatewood
runners.spotlight: A little better when beaten 36l at Ballinrobe last time, but that was nearly a year ago; handicap debut.
runners.comment: A little better at Ballinrobe last time but that was nearly a year ago; handicap debut

runners.name: Sacramental
runners.spotlight: Has shown very little in four maidens; first handicap start and been off since January.
runners.comment: Has shown very little in four maidens; first handicap start

runners.name: Road To The Sea
runners.spotlight: Big prices and heavy defeats in a bumper and three maiden hurdles; might be a different story in first handicap, but can't be fancied on form.
runners.comment: Mare well held in maidens and has been off since November

runners.name: Blue Moon Boy
runners.spotlight: Dual winner and rated 73 on the Flat; has to be of interest off 93 in this sphere after a warm-up on the Flat in March.
runners.comment: Dual winner and rated 73 on the Flat; has to be of interest off 93 in this sphere

races.race_name: Johnston Farm Equipment Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: Maiden out of her depth in rated race on latest and in poor form before that; however, she was beaten 0.5l off 10lb higher over C&D (good) last June and her course form figures read 42242; could revive.
runners.comment: Maiden, out of depth latest, course form figures read 42242, could revive

runners.name: Button Rock
runners.spotlight: Placed in a point and with a top yard but yet to fire under rules; heavy defeat on handicap debut at Down Royal in March when 13-2; blinkers reached for now.
runners.comment: Top yard but yet to show much, heavy defeat on h'cap debut latest, blinkers on

runners.name: Dunany Point
runners.spotlight: Won off 9lb lower at Bellewstown last April (20.5f, good); struggled on soft ground both starts this year but could improve for quicker conditions and ran well over C&D off 2lb lower last May; interesting.
runners.comment: Soft no use to him both starts this year, better ground now, has C&D form, interesting

runners.name: Ladiam
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come here over an extended 2m4f; 3lb above last win here in June 2025 and off since a modest Thurles win in October; will improve for this run.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner, 3lb above last win, off since October, come on for this run

runners.name: Jumble Light
