runners.spotlight: Second reserve; shaped with promise in maidens over shorter (including here when a staying on fifth over 17.5f); pulled up when upped to this trip at Down Royal in March; capable of better and could go well for local yard if getting a run.
runners.comment: Pulled up on latest, capable of better, could go well if getting a run; reserve

runners.name: Mother Ship
runners.spotlight: 0-15 over hurdles; good third over C&D last June off 1lb higher; off since respectable fifth at Thurles in October; has run well fresh and top rider booked so not dismissed.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D last June, has gone well fresh, top rider booked, not dismissed

runners.name: Ilbdare
runners.spotlight: Scored over 3m2f here last May on good ground; placed again here over 25.5f in October off his revised mark; below best when last seen in January and this trip may be too sharp.
runners.comment: Has won and placed here over 3m2f, off since January, this trip looks too sharp

runners.name: Lariat
runners.spotlight: Flat winner is 0-26 over hurdles; off since heavy defeat in handicap chase at Thurles in October; has run well here on the Flat in the past but overall record fresh suggests he might need this after 194 days off.
runners.comment: 0-26 over hurdles, has run well here on the Flat, may need this after 194 days off

runners.name: Listentomewillya
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; minor promise in qualifying runs; potential improver on handicap debut so check the betting.
runners.comment: Glimmers of promise in maidens, potential improver, check market; reserve

runners.name: Golden Order
runners.spotlight: Has looked limited when completing in maiden hurdles; pulled up at Limerick when last seen in November; hood and tongue tie on for handicap debut; unlikely.
runners.comment: Pulled up when last seen in Nov', headgear goes on for handicap debut, unlikely

runners.name: Tomas O Maille
runners.spotlight: 1lb below last April's Ballinrobe win but out of form when last seen in November and may need this after 173 days off.
runners.comment: Out of form when last seen in November, might need this after 173 days off

runners.name: Celtic Crossing
runners.spotlight: Off the track since 31l defeat at Punchestown in October 2024; likely to need this run after such a long layoff.
runners.comment: Off since October 2024, likely to need this run after such a long layoff

runners.name: Road To The Weir
runners.spotlight: Maiden has gone the wrong way since a promising third at Limerick in November 2024; heavy defeat when last seen in December; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Maiden, heavy defeat when last seen in December, hard to fancy

runners.name: Island McCoo
runners.spotlight: Chase winner is rated 4lb lower in this sphere but that's for good reason; five previous handicap hurdle runs have yielded form figures of 80P98; beaten 23l in Thurles handicap chase when last seen in February.
runners.comment: Chase winner, form figures in h'cap hurdles read 80P98, beaten 23l when last seen in Feb'

runners.name: Doyen Wolf
runners.spotlight: First reserve; best run under Rules a 5.5l fifth of 11 over this C&D (gd-yld) in October after a 285 day break and 4lb lower now; pulled up when last sighted at Fairyhouse in January; worth a market check if gets in.
runners.comment: Best effort came over C&D when fresh last Oct', worth market check if gets in; reserve

runners.name: Sunrise Strider
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in first two handicaps on testing ground, over 2m at Limerick in December and 19.5f at Punchestown in January; could go well if acting on better ground; check the market.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in first two h'caps, could go well if handling better ground, check market

runners.name: The Eggler
runners.spotlight: Decent effort on handicap and stable debut at Galway (22.5f, gd-yld) last September but didn't replicate it in two subsequent starts; goes without usual hood here and lacks a recent run; others preferred.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on promising h'cap debut, lacks a recent run, usual hood left off

runners.name: Lady's Choice
runners.spotlight: Promise in a 2m3f maiden hurdle at Downpatrick last May; tailed off on handicap debut off this mark over the same C&D when last sighted last July; best watched.
runners.comment: Tailed off on handicap debut off this mark when last seen in July, best watched

runners.name: Friar Hogan
runners.spotlight: Showed little in maidens and beaten 40l on handicap debut at Thurles last October; 2lb lower now and upped in trip but hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: No show in maidens, beaten 40l on handicap debut, 2lb lower now but hard to fancy

races.race_name: Solar Generation Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ballyglass Beauty
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; last scored in September 2023 but back to form with a 1.25l second at Clonmel on latest; raised 3lb for that but is still 10lb below his last success; C&D form figures read 41343; leading claims.
runners.comment: C&D winner, back to form at Clonmel latest, 10lb below last win, leading claims

runners.name: Survivors Sister
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win at Limerick (2m5f, heavy) in February by 22l off 10lb lower; pulled up and beaten 31l on two starts since; may need softer ground.
runners.comment: Won on heavy at Limerick off 10lb lower, poor twice since, may need it softer
runners.owner: John J McGrath & T C Quinn

runners.name: Gerrys Wish
runners.spotlight: From top yard and knocking on the door last autumn; beaten a neck at Perth in September (3m, good) and a head at Galway in October when last seen (3m, yielding); just 2lb higher now and big chance if fit after a 199 day break.
runners.comment: Top yard, narrow defeats last two starts in the autumn, big chance if fit after break

runners.name: Jumping Susie
runners.spotlight: Flattered by second placing in a 21.5f Tramore beginners' chase on stable debut when benefitting from carnage at the second last fence; however, she was staying on pretty nicely so looks worth a go at this longer trip back over hurdles; visor goes on.
runners.comment: Decent beginners' chase run latest, worth another go at this trip back over timber; visor

runners.name: Mr Dibbs
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner; good second here last August off this mark (20.5f, good); poor form in three points since and jumped poorly when pulled up on return to hurdling at Kilbeggan latest; blinkers reached for.
runners.comment: Good second here last August but poor since, pulled up latest, blinkers tried now

runners.name: Le Grand Vert
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner is rated 15lb lower in this sphere; won over fences at Ayr in February (3m, soft), his second win at that venue; 2lb wrong here though and all four wins on soft and heavy ground.
runners.comment: Rated 15lb lower over hurdles, 2lb wrong here though and best form on soft-heavy

runners.name: Jeriko De Baune
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles, the latest at Clonmel last November off 3lb lower (3m, soft); pulled up on latest but has run well here before so could rebound; place shout.
runners.comment: Last won off 3lb lower in Nov', pulled up latest but has run well here before, place shout
runners.owner: Crohane Town & Country Syndicate/Mrs E A Lawlor

runners.name: Barrowline
runners.spotlight: First sign of ability when hinting that this sort of trip would suit when staying on for a 28l fifth of 16 at Tramore last month; 7lb wrong but that's negated by rider's claim and yard has a decent record here (3-13); could sneak into the money at a price.
runners.comment: Hinted this trip would suit latest, rider's claim a plus, yard has decent record here

runners.name: Portarlington
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles; remans 4lb above his last win and has been off since a heavy defeat at Fairyhouse in November; might need this run.
runners.comment: Remains 4lb above last win, off since heavy defeat in November, may need this run

runners.name: Evans Secret
runners.spotlight: Locally trained; won over C&D (good) off 6lb lower when last seen in July 2025; absence since is concerning and record fresh indicates he will come on for this run.
runners.comment: C&D win off 6lb lower last July, off since, might just need this on return

runners.name: Earl Neigh
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit of promise in qualifying runs over 2m-2m4f on varying ground; big step up in trip could suit on handicap debut and opening mark looks reasonable; lacks a recent run but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Ability in maidens over shorter, step up in trip could suit in first h'cap, check market

runners.name: The Miracle Man
runners.spotlight: Course winner in October 2024 at 20.5f off 91; added a 3m Fairyhouse handicap hurdle (gd-yld) in April 2025 off 10lb higher; has held form pretty well off revised mark since and return to this venue and better ground both positives; player.
runners.comment: Course winner, held form well since 3m Fairyhouse win last April, player
runners.owner: A Trappe & Declan Trappe & Garry Trappe

races.race_name: Lake Isle Of Innisfree INH Flat Race

runners.name: Seven Towers
runners.spotlight: Placed in two points; promise in a Fairyhouse bumper in January (2m, yielding); bombed out on testing ground at Thurles in March; back to form on good to yielding at Kilbeggan latest; should go well again.
runners.comment: Has run well both bumper starts on decent ground, solid third on latest, go well again

runners.name: Bravewave
runners.spotlight: Modest effort on debut at Thurles (2m, soft) in February but more like it when a 7.25l third at Bellewstown (2m1f, yielding) last month at 28-1; big chance if building on that over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Much better effort second time up at Bellewstown, big chance if builds on that
runners.owner: Mrs H Watson & T Martin Murphy & Orla O'Neill

runners.name: Some See Stars
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but stand-out effort came on his only start on decent ground when a 1.25l second at Fairyhouse in January (Seven Towers third); tailed off both starts since on soft and heavy; return to quicker ground could spark a revival.
runners.comment: Good second in January, poor on soft/heavy twice since, may rebound on better ground

runners.name: Mary's Up In Court
runners.spotlight: Fell and pulled up in two points; cheekpieces go on for Rules' debut; impossible to make a case for.
runners.comment: Failed to complete both points, cheekpieces on for Rules' bow, hard to fancy

runners.name: Wild Eagle
runners.spotlight: Wings Of Eagles mare; half-sister to 2m5f hurdle winner Bugscuffle; dam point winner, out of unraced half-sister to useful 2m-3m chase winner Carrickmines; likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Wings Of Eagles mare, dam point winner, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Go To The Moon
runners.spotlight: Bought for 40,000gbp after winning a point for Pierce Power at Stradbally (3m, good) in April 2025; tongue tied for Rules' debut for local yard; market can reveal expectations.
runners.comment: Bought for 40,000gbp after point win, tongue tied for Rules' debut, check market
runners.owner: A Cahill, M Dowd, P Glancy Partnership

runners.name: Bernie's Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Maxios gelding; half-brother to a Flat winner and a bumper and hurdles winner; dam a 1m1f winner in France; not a bad pedigree but yard 0-22 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Maxios gelding, half-brother to a bumper winner, yard 0-22 in this sphere last 5 seasons

runners.name: Counting Coup
runners.spotlight: Vita Venturi gelding; dam point/2m2f hurdle winner; sister to 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Doldido, closely related to prolific 2m6f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner For Jim; yard in form but probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Vita Venturi gelding, dam point/2m2f winner, yard in form but likely best watched

runners.name: Hurricane Leaf
runners.spotlight: Never involved when a 15l seventh of 10 on debut at Listowel (2m, yld-sft) last September; needs more.
runners.comment: Never involved when a 15l seventh of 10 on debut at Listowel last September, needs more.

runners.name: Blaze Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Raven's Pass gelding; half-brother to winners Cinnodin (1m4f-2m Flat inc AW, RPR 84; 2m7f hurdle) and Aiche De Bretel (French 1m1f); dam 1m4f/1m6f winner (96); two of yard's four previous bumper runners here finished second.
runners.comment: Raven's Pass gelding, dam Flat winner, yard has had bumper runners go well here before

runners.name: Jukebox Charlie
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in bumpers at Down Royal and Punchestown; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Tailed off both starts in bumpers, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Carrigeen Kirkia
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in points and no sign of winning potential on Rules' debut in a Kilbeggan bumper last month.
runners.comment: Ordinary point form, struggled in Kilbeggan bumper last month

runners.name: Doreys Forge
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and beaten 45l when fifth on debut at Thurles (16.5f, sft-hvy); could improve for better ground but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on testing ground at Thurles when 40-1, others appeal more

runners.name: In A Sonnet
runners.spotlight: British point winner (2m5f, good) bought for 135,000GBP in April 2025; bitterly disappointing when 6-5 favourite for a Navan bumper in February (2m, heavy); still early days and could improve markedly for better ground; can't discount.
runners.comment: Point winner, bitterly disappointing fav' on heavy at Navan, this ground more suitable

runners.name: Go On Ta
runners.spotlight: 250-1 and failed to beat a rival home on debut at Clonmel in March; put a line through.
runners.comment: 250-1 and last of 8 on debut at Clonmel, put a line through

races.race_name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Arc Zoosve
runners.spotlight: Thriving in 2026 and he gamely made all when completing a hat-trick in 2m Goodwood handicap ten days ago; up another 2lb but this likeable dual C&D scorer is a big player once more.
runners.comment: Completed hat-trick at Goodwood ten days ago; a dual C&D scorer and a big player once more

runners.name: Mio Amico
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark here (1m4f) in January and added a Chelmsford handicap victory (1m6f) in March; below-par fifth off 7lb higher mark at Southwell (1m6f) last time though so more is needed here.
runners.comment: Course winner; below-par fifth at Southwell last time though so a bit more is needed here

runners.name: Polling Day
runners.spotlight: Looked rusty after seven months off when last of seven in 1m4f Wolverhampton handicap last month; this course winner can build on that now.
runners.comment: Looked rusty after seven months off when last at Wolverhampton; can build on it

runners.name: An Bradan Feasa
runners.spotlight: Scored twice over hurdles last autumn and he notched another success in 2m Chelmsford handicap in November; off since but yard is going well so he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Won Chelmsford handicap in November; off since but yard going well so not discounted

runners.name: Bulldog Spirit
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m6f scorer for Brian Ellison in 2025; beat only one for his new handler after seven months off in 1m4f handicap at Wolverhampton last month; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Beat one for new handler at Wolverhampton last month; needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: The Truant
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m at Tramore last August for Paul Flynn; yet to reach that form for his new stable, just fourth of five in 1m6f handicap at Southwell 19 days ago; more is required despite an easing mark.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top form for new stable, fourth of five at Southwell 19 days ago; more required

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Always Happy
runners.spotlight: Justified odds-on favouritism when beating a subsequent winner (pair clear) on her Southwell debut (1m, Tapeta) in March; more to do under her penalty, but clearly highly regarded (holds a Coronation Stakes entry) and respected under Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut at Southwell in March; holds a Coronation Stakes entry; respected
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: York Tower
runners.spotlight: Beaten a length in both starts over 7f late last year, at Yarmouth (good) and Chelmsford (Polytrack); extra furlong should suit and high on list on his return.
runners.comment: Beaten a length in both starts late last year; extra furlong should suit; high on list

runners.name: Savvy Disko
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two starts at Newmarket (7f, good) in October (debut form worked out well); closely related to an AW winner and each-way claims back from seven months off.
runners.comment: Made the frame in two starts at Newmarket in October; each-way claims

runners.name: Turret
runners.spotlight: 580,000euros yearling; dam unraced sister to Group 1 winners Hydrangea (1m/1m4f), The United States (Australian 1m2f) and Hermosa (1,000 Guineas/Irish 1,000 Guineas), out of 5f Group 2 winner; beautifully bred, but was withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Kempton last month (5-2 at the time) and the outside draw may not be ideal for a newcomer.
runners.comment: Withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Kempton last month; outside draw not ideal

runners.name: Molo Del Palazzo
runners.spotlight: Sent off 11-2 when fifth on nine on her Wolverhampton debut (7f, Tapeta) last September; improvement needed, but it is possible with the longer trip likely to suit on breeding.
runners.comment: Fifth on her Wolverhampton debut last September; improvement needed, but it is possible

runners.name: Tropical Sands
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 1m2f/1m3f winner Calmly (including AW/Italian Listed; RPR 99), half-sister to winners Shrewdness (9.4f AW; 85) and Stimulate (1m2f; 77); dam 1m2f winner (86), half-sister to Italian 1m4f Group 1 winner Shamdala; stable had a well-backed 3yo newcomer go in at Newbury last month so the market may be informative.
runners.comment: Stable had a well-backed 3yo newcomer go in at Newbury last month; market informative
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Cormorant Rock
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling; first foal; dam French 1m5f AW winner, half-sister to winners Almanera (5.5f-7f) and Al Capdamunt (6.7f), out of maiden half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Naaqoos; best watched on debut unless market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Best watched on debut unless market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Don Pablo Colina
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam prolific 1m2f-2m Flat (including AW; RPR 82) and 2m hurdle winner, sister to 7f-1m4f winner Chilworth Bells; stable going well, but he may be one for handicaps over further in due course.
runners.comment: Stable going well, but he may be one for handicaps over further in due course

runners.name: Minerality
runners.spotlight: 45,000gns foal; half-brother to winners Cathedral (Group-placed 6f AW 2yo; RPR 118), Tolstoy (6f-1m including AW; 103), Firebird (6f; 99) and Balalaika (7f AW 2yo; 92); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Bluestocking; eyecatching pedigree and stable can get them ready first time; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Eyecatching pedigree and stable can get them ready first time; worth a market check

runners.name: Notable Charm
runners.spotlight: 150-1 when out the back on his Newbury debut (1m, good) last month; needs major improvement.
runners.comment: Out the back on his Newbury debut last month; needs major improvement

runners.name: Midnight Steppers
runners.spotlight: Beaten a very long way in both starts over 1m on turf/Polytrack last month; makes little appeal.
runners.comment: Beaten a very long way in both starts last month; makes little appeal

runners.name: Dupont Circle
runners.spotlight: 125-1 when a long last of ten on his belated Chelmsford debut (7f, Polytrack) in December; no appeal.
runners.comment: Long last of ten on his belated Chelmsford debut in December; no appeal
runners.owner: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eskimo Pie
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful maiden at two when an excellent seventh in Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; returned with a good third in 1m Kempton novice in March but well below par when sixth in similar company at Nottingham (1m) 24 days ago; holds leading form claims if back to her best.
runners.comment: Fairly useful; below-par Nottingham sixth latest; leading form claims if back to best

runners.name: Bright Summer
runners.spotlight: 11-1, promising fourth of 11 in maiden at Newbury (7f, good) 25 days ago, leading until 1f out; that form is working out well so this daughter of Dark Angel is a player with better to come.
runners.comment: Promising debut fourth at Newbury; form franked so she's a player with better to come

runners.name: Music Academy
runners.spotlight: Kingman filly who took a big step forward from her debut when third of seven in 7f Kempton maiden last month; she can do better still so needs considering.
runners.comment: Big step forward with Kempton third last month; can do better still so needs considering

runners.name: Thursday Girl
runners.spotlight: Built on debut promise when second of five in 7f Newmarket maiden 26 days ago, making most; this daughter of Pinatubo is open to more progress here.
runners.comment: Good second at Newmarket 26 days ago; this daughter of Pinatubo is open to more progress

runners.name: Melting Snow
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and green when a never-dangerous eighth of ten in maiden at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) on her debut 17 days ago; this Too Darn Hot filly can do better in the longer term.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous eighth at Doncaster on debut; she can do better in the longer term

runners.name: Diane Chasseresse
runners.spotlight: Havana Grey filly; half-sister to 7f-2m winner Alfred Boucher (RPR 109); dam, unraced half-sister to winners Mores Wells (1m2f-1m4f Group 3) and Galient (useful 1m4f Flat and 2m5f/3m hurdle), is out of a 2m Group 3 winner; appeals on paper and is a much respected newcomer; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Havana Grey filly who is a much respected newcomer; worth a market check

runners.name: Hatour
runners.spotlight: 7-1, shaped well on her debut when a running-on fourth of 11 in maiden at Newmarket (7f, good) in October; this Sioux Nation filly looks to have a fair bit more to offer in 2026.
runners.comment: Shaped well when debut fourth at Newmarket in October; looks to have more to offer

runners.name: Lesley Buckley
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three runs for Hayley Burton at two and fared no better for her new stable in 6f Kempton maiden eight days ago; visor is reached for.
runners.comment: Showed little at two and fared no better for new yard on her return at Kempton

runners.name: Victress
runners.spotlight: Modest form shown in a pair of 6f novices at Wolverhampton and Southwell before Christmas; plenty more is required from this Iffraaj filly after a layoff.
runners.comment: Modest form shown at Wolverhampton and Southwell before Christmas; plenty more is required

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Thestral
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a Newcastle novice (7f, Tapeta) in December, but having been gelded his two efforts since returning in March leave plenty to be desired; needs a resurgence in the returning blinkers (worn two starts ago).
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in December, but two efforts since returning leave plenty to be desired

runners.name: Slack Bob
runners.spotlight: Gradually getting there, going down by half a length at Yarmouth (1m, good) last month on his return from 89 days off; that performance suggested the return to this trip would prove beneficial; major player.
runners.comment: Just beaten over 1m at Yarmouth last time and the return to 7f should suit; major player
runners.owner: The Old Etonian Racing Syndicate II

runners.name: Lovethiswayagain
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over 6f here on her return in January, but hasn't kicked on since; stamina for this far remains unproven and she needs the first-time blinkers to make a difference.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 6f here on her return in January, but hasn't kicked on since; blinkers on

runners.name: Roccobear
runners.spotlight: Best effort when beaten less than a length on handicap debut over C&D in March; twice held since, though latest Kempton effort forgivable on account of the draw; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Best effort when beaten under a length on handicap debut over C&D in March; shortlisted

runners.name: Grey Force
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability as when beaten just over a length into fourth of nine on handicap/turf debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) ten days ago; still to truly convince over this far, though.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability, but is still to truly convince over this far

runners.name: Karmacy
runners.spotlight: Well held in four starts over 7f-8.6f on turf/AW for the Newland/Insole yard; market may indicate what is expected on stable debut after seven weeks off.
runners.comment: Well held in four starts; market may indicate what is expected on stable debut

runners.name: Miss Starlet
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when in the frame in her last three starts on AW/turf as a 2yo; market may prove useful on her return from 167 days off, but she may need further than this now.
runners.comment: Market may prove useful on return from 167 days off but she may need further than this now

runners.name: Fuseboard
runners.spotlight: 250-1 for all four starts over 6f-1m on the AW, being well beaten each time; makes his handicap debut after four months off having been gelded and undergone wind surgery; best watched.
runners.comment: 250-1 for all four starts over 6f-1m on the AW, being well beaten each time; best watched

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap

runners.name: Signcastle City
runners.spotlight: Was back off the same mark as for his Chelmsford success (7f, Polytrack) in February when second of six at Bath (1m, firm) 13 days ago; back up 2lb but he can't be left out of calculations.
runners.comment: Returned to form when runner-up at Bath 13 days ago; can't be left out of calculations

runners.name: Metallo
runners.spotlight: 0-13 in handicaps and usually seen over further than this, but he did run well when fourth of nine over 7f here in March; with a couple in here that like to go forward, he may have the race run to suit and drops into a 0-80 for the first time; hood removed; interesting.
runners.comment: Usually seen over further, but he may have the race run to suit and drops in class

runners.name: Studious
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come on Tapeta including at Southwell (1m) five weeks ago, but he does handle Polytrack; respected despite a 4lb rise with cheekpieces now given a go.
runners.comment: Four wins on Tapeta including last time, but he does handle Polytrack; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Zatsgood
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in his first two starts on the AW over 16 months apart, the first in France; ran well against the 3yos when fourth of ten in a Kempton maiden (1m, Polytrack) last month and open to further progress now handicapping.
runners.comment: In the frame in all three starts and open to further progress now handicapping

runners.name: Special Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Made all for both wins at around 1m on turf last year, each for a different stable; has run well on Tapeta, but Polytrack remains a question and he will need to do better than on his debut for this yard at Bath 15 days ago.
runners.comment: Polytrack still a question and he needs to do better than on his debut for this yard

runners.name: Frostmagic
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who has mainly run well since the autumn, including a win at Southwell (1m, Tapeta) in January and a good third of ten on last month's Beverley return (7f, good to firm); shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Handy sort who has mainly run well since the autumn; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Buy The Dip
runners.spotlight: 2-3 over C&D including when making a successful return from 196 days off 12 days ago; up 7lb in a better race so he will need to find a bit more.
runners.comment: 2-3 over C&D including a win 12 days ago; up 7lb in a better race; has to find a bit more

runners.name: Dontspoilasale
runners.spotlight: Five wins in Ireland, three of them at Dundalk (6f, Polytrack); market useful on stable debut, but this trip is an unknown.
runners.comment: Five wins in Ireland; market useful on stable debut, but this trip is an unknown

runners.name: Zoffandia
runners.spotlight: Responded well to the fitting of a visor when winning easily on his Kempton return (1m, Polytrack) in February, but twice disappointing since and still 7lb higher; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Won easily at Kempton in February, but twice disappointing since and still 7lb higher

runners.name: Baltic Voyage
runners.spotlight: 1lb below sole winning mark, but very lightly raced since joining this yard and failed to beat a rival at Newbury (1m1f, good) last month; probably best watched on AW debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced for this yard and failed to beat a rival last time; best watched on AW debut

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Bela Sonata
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo whose record last year read 131 including a win in a Kempton maiden (7f, Polytrack); needs to put a poor performance on her return at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) in January well behind her, though, and she has been off another four months since.
runners.comment: Needs to put a poor performance on her return at Newcastle in January well behind her

runners.name: Queue Dos
runners.spotlight: All four wins at Wolverhampton, including one over this trip, but has run well on Polytrack; should be all the better for last month's return when fourth of six over 6f back at Wolverhampton (first and third have won since); a player.
runners.comment: Has won over this far and should be all the better for last month's reappearance; a player

runners.name: Barefoot Beach
runners.spotlight: All three starts over 6f on the AW, winning at Kempton (Polytrack) last September and finishing runner-up in both starts since returning in March; shouldn't have a problem with the extra furlong on breeding and well worth a second look.
runners.comment: Record so far reads 122; shouldn't have a problem with the extra furlong on breeding

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: Five wins, four on the AW including over C&D last month; beaten less than a length, albeit only fifth of nine, back here 12 days ago; better drawn than of late and could go well.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month; better drawn than of late and could go well

runners.name: Handle With Care
runners.spotlight: Seemed to have no excuses here last time, but is back off the same mark as when making all over C&D in December; respected if allowed her own way again.
runners.comment: Off same mark as when making all over C&D in December; respected if allowed her own way

runners.name: Brisk Symphony
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f on fast turf last summer and, having been off six months, ran well to finish second of eight on her Yarmouth return (7f, good to firm) 12 days ago; considered if taking to this new surface.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner last year and second on return; considered if taking to the new surface

runners.name: Moonlight Mirage
runners.spotlight: Wolverhampton novice winner (5f, Tapeta) for George Scott last September, but failed to beat a rival on her stable debut/reappearance at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) last month; enough to prove, not least stamina.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival on her stable debut at Southwell last month; enough to prove

runners.name: Ghisa
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in Tapeta novices (6f/7f) for Ollie Pears in the autumn, but held in three starts on turf/dirt for this yard at Meydan; hard to know how well handicapped she is; tongue-tie now given a go.
runners.comment: 2-2 in Tapeta novices in the autumn, but held in three starts at Meydan; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Cloudbuster
runners.spotlight: Mainly consistent last year, including a win in a Chepstow maiden (6f, good), and her final effort can be forgiven on account of the draw; has to overcome a 230-day absence, though, and she has the outside stall.
runners.comment: Mainly consistent last year, but has to overcome a 230-day absence and is drawn widest

runners.name: Inside Story
runners.spotlight: Narrowly won a Southwell maiden (6f, Tapeta) last September (form worked out well); twice out of the frame since, but not disgraced on either occasion; extra furlong should suit on pedigree and she's not without a chance.
runners.comment: Southwell maiden win in 2025; extra furlong should suit on pedigree; not without a chance

runners.name: Bami
runners.spotlight: Consistent in four starts late last year, including winning a novice over C&D; worthy of consideration back from five months off, especially if attracting support.
runners.comment: Consistent in four starts late last year, including winning a novice over C&D; considered

runners.name: The Third Star
runners.spotlight: Started off last year in good form for Chris Dwyer including winning a Southwell maiden (7f, Tapeta); regressed afterwards, albeit she faced some stiff tasks; market useful on stable debut (same owners) after seven months off.
runners.comment: Faced stiff tasks after starting off last season well; watch market on stable debut/return

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Lightning Tiger
runners.spotlight: Fifth in his first two starts on the AW, but has improved since including when making a successful handicap debut at Catterick (1m4f, soft) in October; creditable fourth of 12 on his Thirsk reappearance (1m4f, good) last month (third has won since); could go well in the first-time hood.
runners.comment: Has won over this trip on turf; last month's Thirsk reappearance has been franked; hood on

runners.name: Twilight Moon
runners.spotlight: Has won her last two starts over C&D 133 days apart; up another 3lb in her hat-trick bid and she has to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Has won her last two starts over C&D 133 days apart; has to be taken very seriously

runners.name: Arth's Gold
runners.spotlight: Made it 14th-time lucky at Southwell (1m4f) a fortnight ago, making his record on Tapeta 5364321; record on Polytrack reads 79657, though, which would be more of a concern than the 6lb rise.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell last time, but his record on Polytrack reads 79657 which is a concern

runners.name: Yaa Min
runners.spotlight: Narrowly made a successful stable/handicap debut on his reappearance over C&D in March; twice held over shorter trips since, but interesting back here from just 2lb higher than for his win.
runners.comment: Just 2lb higher than when winning over C&D in March; interesting back here

runners.name: Victors Spirit
runners.spotlight: Has improved since handicapping on the AW with a hood enlisted, beating a solid yardstick at Southwell (1m3f, Tapeta) last month and finishing second of nine over 1m2f here nine days later; should be suited by the return to further; respected.
runners.comment: Has improved since handicapping on the AW; should be suited by this return to further
runners.owner: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership

runners.name: Penzance
runners.spotlight: Has been a smart performer in his time (rated 18lb higher), but well below form in his last two starts including on stable debut here (1m2f) last month; tongue-tie removed after one use; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: A smart performer in his time, but well below form in last two starts; look elsewhere

runners.name: Epictetus
runners.spotlight: Beaten half a length or less in his last three goes on the AW (7f-9.5f), but losing run up to 22 and has never attempted this far before; others safer.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 22 and has never attempted this far before; others safer

runners.name: Rogue Impact
runners.spotlight: Landed a C&D maiden just over a year ago, but his form since then has been patchy; down another 3lb, but more needed if he is to resume winning ways.
runners.comment: C&D winner just over a year ago, but his form since then has been patchy; more needed

races.race_name: CRE RUN FARM HANDICAP STAKES 0-95
